145		}))
146		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
147		runCmd(t, root, "git", "init")
148		runCmd(t, root, "git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
149		runCmd(t, root, "git", "config", "user.name", "Test User")
150		runCmd(t, root, "git", "add", ".")
151		runCmd(t, root, "git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "init")
152		return root
153	}
154	
155	func TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook(t *testing.T) {
156		root := setupWorkerIntakeFixture(t)
157		stub := &workerIntakeServiceStub{}
158		installWorkerIntakeStub(t, stub)
159	
160		m := NewWorkerManager(root)
161		record, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
162			ProjectRoot: root,
163			Harness:     "claude",
164			Mode:        "once",
165			NoReview:    true,
166		})
167		require.NoError(t, err)
168		_ = waitForWorkerExit(t, m, record.ID, 10*time.Second)
169	
170		stub.mu.Lock()
171		got := append([]string(nil), stub.modes...)
172		stub.mu.Unlock()
173	
174		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 2, "server worker must invoke intake and lint hooks")
175		assert.Equal(t, "intake", got[0], "server-managed workers must run intake before claim")
176		assert.Equal(t, "lint", got[1], "server-managed workers must wire lint after intake")
177	}
178	
179	// TestServerWorker_ReadinessUnavailableEvidence (AC2, AC6 / ddx-30bc30ed):
180	// when a server-managed worker encounters an intake service failure, it must
181	// record readiness-unavailable evidence in the worker log. The test fails if
182	// that evidence is dropped.
183	func TestServerWorker_ReadinessUnavailableEvidence(t *testing.T) {
184		root := setupWorkerIntakeFixture(t)
185		stub := &workerIntakeServiceStub{
186			intakeErr: fmt.Errorf("readiness service unavailable: connection refused"),
187		}
188		installWorkerIntakeStub(t, stub)
189	
190		m := NewWorkerManager(root)
191		record, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
192			ProjectRoot: root,
193			Harness:     "claude",
194			Mode:        "once",
195			NoReview:    true,
196		})
197		require.NoError(t, err)
198		_ = waitForWorkerExit(t, m, record.ID, 10*time.Second)
199	
200		// AC2: worker log must contain actionable readiness-unavailable evidence.
201		logContent, _, err := m.Logs(record.ID)
202		require.NoError(t, err)
203		assert.Contains(t, logContent, "readiness check unavailable",
204			"server worker must record readiness-unavailable evidence in worker log; got: %s", logContent)
205	}

input.content:
status: proposed
reason: fizeau v0.13.1 integration in code is already merged (commit 325c45b35 "chore(agent): update fizeau to v0.13.1" — bumps cli/go.mod from v0.13.0 to v0.13.1). AC#1 already passes against HEAD: `grep -rEn "github.com/easel/fizeau (v0\.13\.[1-9]|v0\.1[4-9])" cli/go.mod cli/go.sum` returns 3 matches. The remaining ACs require operator-side actions outside this isolated execution worktree:

- AC#2 ("installed `ddx` binary on PATH carries v0.13.1 symbol `ProviderUnreachable`") needs a rebuild + install of the ddx binary onto the operator host's PATH. The execution worktree under /var/tmp cannot write to ~/.local/bin or wherever the operator's `ddx` binary lives.
- AC#3 (5-minute soak with `ddx work --watch` against a deliberately-off provider) requires restarting long-running daemon processes and pointing them at a real (off) host, then observing ~/Projects/ddx/.ddx/logs/ddx-server.log over 5 minutes. Both the daemon lifecycle and the log path live outside the worktree.
- AC#4 (`go test ./cli/... -count=1 -timeout 5m` exits 0 in ~/Projects/ddx) was attempted in the worktree after `make frontend-build`. Two unrelated failures observed, neither caused by the fizeau bump: (a) `internal/server` -short hits `TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook` failing `expected "lint" got "triage"` — a worker-hook ordering regression in workers.go that pre-dates this bead and belongs to the worker-intake area; (b) without -short, `TestMCPAgentModels` and other provider-probe tests hang on real network calls (they are gated by `testing.Short()` and only run in non-short mode). Neither failure references fizeau routing or the v0.13.1 changes.

Operator action required: `cd ~/Projects/ddx/cli && make build install` (or equivalent), restart any `ddx work --watch` / `ddx server` processes, run the 5-minute soak, then run AC#4's full test command. Pre-existing `TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook` failure should be filed as a separate bead (workers.go hook ordering) — out of scope here.

data.output: File created successfully at: /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c2e7f14b-20260514T092041-0bfe506b/.ddx/executions/20260514T092041-0bfe506b/no_changes_rationale.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

data.text:
Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `status: proposed`. Summary:

- **Code change is already merged.** Commit `325c45b35` ("chore(agent): update fizeau to v0.13.1") bumped `cli/go.mod` to v0.13.1. AC#1's grep returns 3 matches against HEAD.
- **AC#2 / AC#3 require operator action outside the worktree** — installing the binary onto PATH and restarting long-running `ddx work --watch` / `ddx server` daemons, then a 5-minute log soak against a deliberately-off provider.
- **AC#4 has unrelated pre-existing failures.** After `make frontend-build`, `internal/server -short` fails on `TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook` (expects "lint", gets "triage" — workers.go hook-ordering regression unrelated to fizeau). Non-short mode hangs on `TestMCPAgentModels` real-network probes.

No source edits were made — all routing changes live inside fizeau v0.13.1, which the go.mod pin already pulls in.
